items.name: Steam Machine
items.summary: Steam Machine is Valve's living-room PC for the Steam ecosystem.
items.why_included: The launch thread drew unusually practical discussion around price and Linux support.
items.evidence: Commenters focused on the base price, Fedora/Linux support, and whether the machine fills a real desktop gap.

items.name: Deno Desktop
items.summary: Deno Desktop is Deno's runtime for building desktop apps.
items.why_included: Commenters liked the smaller-binary path and the cleaner desktop-app workflow.
items.evidence: Replies discussed the shared CEF runtime, binary size reduction, and how this could make desktop app builds less clunky.

items.name: Oak
items.source_story_title: Show HN: Oak – Git alternative designed for agents
items.summary: Oak is a git alternative built around agent workspaces.
items.why_included: The comments liked the lazy-mount model and the multi-repo workspace story for agents.
items.evidence: Commenters said git worktrees are a pain and praised the checkout and workspace model for multi-agent jobs.

items.name: OpenCode
items.summary: OpenCode is an open-source, model-neutral AI coding agent.
items.why_included: The GLM-vs-Opus thread surfaced it as a real-world coding-agent alternative.
items.evidence: A commenter said they had been using OpenCode, and the discussion shifted into how people mix coding agents with different models.

items.name: Moebius
items.source_story_title: Moebius: 0.2B image inpainting model with 10B-level performance
items.summary: Moebius is a tiny image inpainting model with surprisingly strong demos.
items.why_included: Commenters were impressed by the sample gallery even while debating the headline performance claim.
items.evidence: Replies pointed to the demo spaces and sample images; one noted it felt strong for a 0.2B model.

items.name: Olinia
items.source_story_title: Mexican government unveils a prototype for a new homegrown, ultra-affordable EV
items.summary: Olinia is a Mexico-first ultra-affordable EV prototype.
items.why_included: Commenters treated it as a real local-market program rather than a generic concept car.
items.evidence: Replies linked the company site and emphasized that it is designed in Mexico for Mexico.

items.name: wiggle3ds
items.summary: wiggle3ds is a web app that auto-converts 3DS photos into wigglegrams.
items.why_included: Commenters shared it as an easy automatic workflow instead of manual wiggle editing.
items.evidence: The thread linked wiggle3ds alongside other wigglegram tools and custom-camera setups.

items.name: BlueSCSI
items.source_story_title: Nintendo Wii U games running from a 1980's Bernoulli disk [video]
items.summary: BlueSCSI is SCSI emulation hardware for retro machines.
items.why_included: The retro-storage thread turned into a practical hardware recommendation, and BlueSCSI was the concrete link.
items.evidence: A commenter linked bluescsi.com while reminiscing about SCSI setups and old storage media.

items.name: Nonoverse
items.source_story_title: Show HN: Got sick of ads, so I made my own logic puzzle site
items.summary: Nonoverse is a no-ads nonogram app for iPhone, iPad, and Mac.
items.why_included: A commenter recommended it as the clean offline option for image logic puzzles.
items.evidence: The reply linked the developer site and App Store page, calling out offline play, dark mode, and no accounts.
